aliases: how to run a database on Kubernetes

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Kubernetes 1.27: StatefulSet start ordinals (2023-04); Kubernetes 1.31: configurable ordinal ranges GA (2024-08)

constraints: StatefulSets REQUIRE a headless Service (clusterIP: None) -- without it, Pods have no stable DNS names for replication
volumeClaimTemplates CANNOT be modified after StatefulSet creation -- plan storage size and class before deploying
PersistentVolumeClaims are NOT automatically deleted when StatefulSet is scaled down or deleted -- orphaned PVCs accumulate cost
NEVER use Deployment for databases that require stable network identity or ordered startup (e.g., primary/replica topology)
Database backups MUST use the database's native dump tool (pg_dump, mysqldump, mongodump) -- filesystem-level PVC snapshots can produce corrupt backups

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Running a stateless web application or microservice without persistent data
Need to autoscale application Pods based on CPU/memory
Using a managed database service (RDS, Cloud SQL, Azure Database)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use your cloud provider's managed database documentation

inputs_needed.question: Which database engine are you deploying?
Do you want to manage the database manually or use an operator?
How large is your platform/DevOps team?
